text.title: Node.js Service Layer Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying nodejs-service-layer. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Controller has 250 lines including validation, multiple DB calls, conditional rendering.
antipattern.root-cause: Author kept adding 'one more thing' until controller became the whole feature.
antipattern.fix: Extract into service. Controller reduces to decode + service.x(...) + encode.

antipattern.symptom: Controller imports PrismaClient and queries directly to bypass service.
antipattern.fix: Add repo + service method. Controller goes through service. Add lint rule blocking @prisma/* imports outside repo files.

antipattern.symptom: Service imports `express.Request` to read a header from a request object passed by controller.
antipattern.root-cause: Author shortcut value extraction at controller boundary.
antipattern.fix: Extract values at controller. Service takes primitives. Add depcheck/import-linter rule.

antipattern.symptom: Repo declares interface with 30 methods; service uses 4 of them.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wrote interface at repo to 'expose surface'.
antipattern.fix: Move interfaces to consumer (service) side. Repo exports concrete class; service declares the methods it needs.
